examples.title: 1980: the hinge year
examples.subtitle: Late analog culture starts mutating into postmodern surface, portable media, style magazines, and video-era self-fashioning.
primaryLens: postmodernism becomes public architecture
new wave and post-punk replace punk as the dominant style engine
personal electronics turn media into a private environment
fashion, music, film, and graphic design become one feedback loop
pre-digital tools begin producing a digital-looking visual language

recipes.name: Post-punk archive
recipes.useFor: memory tools, writing apps, research systems, local-first software, archives, personal knowledge tools.
recipes.palette: black, bone, concrete, deep red.
recipes.type: restrained serif or grotesk, wide tracking, sparse hierarchy.
recipes.layout: strict grid with one destabilizing element.
recipes.imagery: monochrome object photography, cropped documents, archival fragments.
recipes.motion: slow reveal, fade, mechanical page change.

recipes.name: New Wave operating manual
recipes.useFor: creative tools, music software, experimental interfaces, developer tools with personality.
recipes.palette: white, black, cyan, red-orange, plastic yellow.
recipes.type: condensed sans, rotated labels, symbol bullets, asymmetric composition.
recipes.layout: grid visible but broken.
recipes.imagery: cutout photography, halftone, primitive digital masks.
recipes.motion: snap cuts, rhythmic panels, jumpy transitions.

recipes.name: Portable private world
recipes.useFor: audio products, journaling, wearables, focus tools, personal AI companions.
recipes.palette: silver, blue, charcoal, soft black, small red indicators.
recipes.type: technical sans, small caps, mechanical labels.
recipes.layout: object-first, tactile controls, compact modules.
recipes.imagery: hands, streets, headphones, transit, pockets.
recipes.motion: button depressions, tape movement, LED pulses.

recipes.name: Pre-Memphis threshold
recipes.useFor: brand refreshes, furniture/product concepts, playful AI tools, education products.
recipes.palette: primary colors plus black-white pattern.
recipes.type: simple geometric forms, not overly wacky.
recipes.layout: objects as symbols, components as characters.
recipes.imagery: laminate, squiggle, block, arch, stepped shape.
recipes.motion: theatrical reveals, object entrances, stage-like transitions.
recipes.risk: accidentally using 1981-1987 Memphis as a lazy shorthand.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: more Alchimia critique, less Saved-by-the-Bell pastiche.

promptSeeds:
Design this as if it were made in 1980, at the exact moment when Swiss typography
starts absorbing post-punk, cassette culture, and primitive computer graphics.
Keep it analog, severe, stylish, and slightly unstable.

Give me three 1980-informed directions:
1. Post-punk archive
2. New Wave operating manual
3. Portable private world
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, and what to avoid.

Critique this interface through a 1980 lens. Where is it too frictionless?
Where could it use stronger materiality, identity, rhythm, or cultural tension?

objects: Zines, flyers, Letraset sheets, paste-up boards
Chrome/glass furniture and soft neutral tailoring
printAndGraphics: The Face, first issue and early identity
Talking Heads - Remain in Light sleeve
David Bowie - Scary Monsters (and Super Creeps) visuals
spaces: Early-80s record shops, arcades, and clubs

antiCliches: MTV-era visual saturation as if it already exists
Aerobics mall graphics unless the brief specifically wants mass-market mid-80s
Pixel art that looks like late-1980s/1990s game nostalgia

sectionMap.label: Year thesis
sectionMap.description: modernism losing its monopoly

sectionMap.label: Graphic design
sectionMap.description: Graphic design in 1980 is split between austerity and signal overload.

sectionMap.label: Product design

sectionMap.label: Architecture
sectionMap.description: Architecture in 1980 is arguing in public.

sectionMap.label: Fashion

sectionMap.label: Music
sectionMap.description: 1980 music is the sound of punk's explosion reorganizing itself.

examples.title: 1981: the broadcast interface
examples.subtitle: Memphis debuts, MTV goes on air, desktop computing becomes a commercial design problem, and style starts moving at television speed.
primaryLens: postmodern object language becomes visible at scale
music becomes a moving-image identity system
personal computing splits into beige standardization and graphical metaphor
New Romantic and Japanese avant-garde fashion redraw the body
film design turns nostalgia, urban decay, and neon modernity into usable worlds

recipes.name: Mutable broadcast identity
recipes.useFor: media brands, AI assistants, creative tools, music products, launch campaigns.
recipes.palette: black, white, electric blue, hot pink, acid green.
recipes.type: massive block logo plus graffiti/handmade interruption.
recipes.layout: simple core mark, endlessly changing surface.
recipes.imagery: texture fills, video stills, broadcast artifacts, fast edits.
recipes.motion: morph, wipe, smash cut, analog glitch, logo costume changes.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: a stable silhouette under all variation.

recipes.name: Memphis object-system
recipes.useFor: playful hardware, education tools, furniture, toy-like interfaces, optimistic brand systems.
recipes.palette: turquoise, red, yellow, black-white pattern, lavender.
recipes.type: simple geometric labels, not novelty fonts.
recipes.layout: asymmetric blocks, object-like modules, pattern fields.
recipes.imagery: laminate, ceramic, shelves, lamps, arches, columns, terrazzo/speckle.
recipes.motion: theatrical object entrances, parts stacking, pattern swaps.

recipes.name: Beige platform future
recipes.useFor: developer tools, databases, enterprise AI, documentation systems, productivity products.
recipes.palette: beige, cream, warm grey, green CRT, black text.
recipes.type: monospaced labels, system sans, dot-matrix output.
recipes.layout: modular panels, forms, folders, tables, expandable slots.
recipes.imagery: keyboards, CRTs, manuals, disks, cables, printer paper.
recipes.motion: cursor blink, boot sequence, fan hum, print feed.

recipes.name: Black architectural fashion
recipes.useFor: fashion brands, privacy tools, serious AI companions, writing environments, galleries.
recipes.palette: black, charcoal, ink, off-white, shadow.
recipes.type: restrained, spacious, slightly severe.
recipes.layout: asymmetry, negative space, concealed structure.
recipes.imagery: fabric texture, volume, folds, unfinished edges.
recipes.motion: slow drape, reveal, conceal, shift in silhouette.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: imperfection, volume, and refusal of body-display norms.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1981 lens: Memphis has just arrived, MTV has just gone on air,
and the personal computer is becoming a business object. Give me a system that can
move between object, screen, logo, and editorial layout.

Give me three 1981-informed directions:
1. Mutable broadcast identity
2. Beige platform future
3. Black architectural fashion
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, interaction,
and what to avoid.

Critique this product as if it launched in 1981. Is it more Memphis object,
MTV identity, IBM platform, Xerox interface, or New Romantic persona?
What would make that lineage stronger?

objects: CRT monitors, keyboards, floppy disks, dot-matrix printers
Decorative laminate, ceramic lamps, shelving totems
printAndGraphics: Early PC manuals and setup diagrams
spaces: MTV control rooms and early cable broadcast environments
Dystopian urban ruins in Escape from New York
Industrial lofts and Parisian pop modernity in Diva

antiCliches: Generic Memphis squiggle wallpaper with no object logic
MTV maximalism without a stable identity structure
Digital smoothness that could only come from later tools

examples.title: 1982: synthetic futures
examples.subtitle: Cyberpunk rain, vector glow, compact discs, home computers, postmodern civic color, and the first real sense that the future has multiple competing skins.
examples.feeling: the future has become a style war
primaryLens: Blade Runner makes the future dense, wet, retrofitted, and multicultural
Tron makes computation visible as black space, glowing geometry, and synthetic motion
home computing becomes mass-market culture through machines like the Commodore 64
digital audio and CAD begin shifting design from analog media into encoded systems
postmodernism leaves the showroom and becomes civic architecture

recipes.name: Retrofitted cyber-noir
recipes.useFor: data tools, memory systems, urban products, security software, archival interfaces, fiction brands.
recipes.palette: wet black, sodium orange, cyan signage, neon red, smoke grey.
recipes.type: dense signage, condensed labels, multilingual overlays, corporate marks.
recipes.layout: layered panels, vertical crowding, hidden infrastructure, street-level detail.
recipes.imagery: rain, vents, cables, ducts, old buildings with new machinery.
recipes.motion: slow scan, flicker, steam reveal, light reflection.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: retrofit logic, not random neon.

recipes.name: Vector ritual
recipes.useFor: games, simulation tools, AI visualizers, technical demos, motion identities.
recipes.palette: black, electric blue, red, white, yellow.
recipes.type: minimal, geometric, glowing, interface-like.
recipes.layout: arenas, grids, lines, discs, directional paths.
recipes.imagery: vector forms, circuitry, luminous suits, abstract vehicles.
recipes.motion: linear paths, snaps, pulses, collisions, transformations.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: early-computer restraint and simple geometry.

recipes.name: Optical digital audio
recipes.useFor: music products, audio apps, archives, media libraries, physical-digital packaging.
recipes.palette: mirror silver, black glass, rainbow edge, fluorescent green.
recipes.type: precise sans, small technical labels, track numbers.
recipes.layout: tray, index, track modularity, circular geometry.
recipes.imagery: disc reflection, laser, jewel case, hi-fi display.
recipes.motion: tray open, spin-up, track skip, laser seek.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: format ritual and physical optics.

recipes.name: Home computer room
recipes.useFor: learning tools, playful dev environments, retro computing brands, education products.
recipes.palette: beige, brown, warm grey, TV blue, black.
recipes.type: monospaced, low-res, manual-like.
recipes.layout: keyboard-first, TV frame, typed commands, cartridge/floppy/cassette modules.
recipes.imagery: manuals, cables, carpet, desk lamps, disks, BASIC prompts.
recipes.motion: boot screen, cursor blink, sprite movement, loading wait.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: domestic plastic hardware and early software awkwardness.

recipes.name: Postmodern civic facade
recipes.useFor: public-sector brands, museums, cultural institutions, civic tech, placemaking.
recipes.palette: terracotta, teal, cream, copper, muted blue-green.
recipes.type: formal but playful, architectural, legible from distance.
recipes.layout: base/middle/top, column-like side structures, central emblem.
recipes.imagery: facade, sculpture, city seal, public threshold.
recipes.motion: slow reveal, facade assembly, color-block transitions.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: symbol, hierarchy, and public legibility.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1982 lens: Blade Runner and Tron have just created two
competing futures, the Commodore 64 has brought computing home, and the compact
disc has made music feel optical and digital. Give me directions that keep those
futures distinct instead of blending them into generic retrofuturism.

Give me three 1982-informed directions:
1. Retrofitted cyber-noir
2. Vector ritual
3. Optical digital audio
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, product logic,
and what to avoid.

Critique this interface as if it launched in 1982. Is it a home computer, a CAD
tool, a Tron-like simulation, a Blade Runner street interface, or a postmodern
civic object? What evidence supports that lineage?

objects: CAD screens, plotter output, and drafting boards
CRT televisions used as computer displays
printAndGraphics: Blade Runner signage and production design
Tron posters, title graphics, and concept art
Michael Jackson - Thriller sleeve and styling
Grandmaster Flash and the Furious Five - "The Message" visual world
spaces: Tron game grid and digital arenas
Portland Building facade and civic lobby

antiCliches: Random Memphis squiggles with no object or postmodern logic
Tron aesthetics with modern LED smoothness
Compact disc nostalgia without acknowledging the format was brand new and expensive

sectionMap.label: 1982 to 1981
sectionMap.description: Year-to-year change.

sectionMap.label: Design climate
sectionMap.description: 1982 is pulled between retrofitted reality and synthetic abstraction.

sectionMap.label: Timeline signals
sectionMap.description: Blade Runner is released, Tron is released, The Commodore 64 is introduced, Time names the...

sectionMap.label: Typography
sectionMap.description: 1982 typography is caught between street density, screen logic, and postmodern quotation.

sectionMap.label: Graphic design
sectionMap.description: 1982 graphic design learns to live across paper, screen, street, sleeve, and object.

sectionMap.label: Product design
sectionMap.description: 1982 product design is where the decade's digital future starts to become domestic and comm...

sectionMap.label: Architecture
sectionMap.description: 1982 architecture is no longer only debating postmodernism. It is building it.

sectionMap.label: Fashion
sectionMap.description: 1982 fashion is about silhouette as signal.

sectionMap.label: Music
sectionMap.description: 1982 music sits at the moment when pop becomes multimedia architecture.

sectionMap.label: Film
sectionMap.description: 1982 is one of the most design-important film years in the corpus.

sectionMap.label: Surface
sectionMap.description: 1982 surfaces often do one of five things:

sectionMap.label: Anti-cliches
sectionMap.description: Do not make 1982 look like:

examples.title: 1983: lifestyle interfaces
examples.subtitle: GUI, MIDI, Swatch, Famicom, mobile phones, MTV pop, and dancewear turn technology and identity into things you wear, play, touch, and perform.
examples.feeling: the interface moves onto the body
primaryLens: interfaces become visible, tactile, and personal
pop image becomes a full audiovisual operating system
design moves from object language into lifestyle accessory
electronic music gains a shared protocol through MIDI
play, portability, and personal technology reshape the domestic future

recipes.name: Friendly GUI office
recipes.useFor: productivity tools, personal AI dashboards, document systems, local-first apps.
recipes.palette: beige, paper white, black, cool grey.
recipes.type: bitmap-like sans, menu labels, document titles.
recipes.layout: windows, icons, folders, desk metaphor, clear hierarchy.
recipes.imagery: mouse, documents, floppy disks, office desktop.
recipes.motion: open window, drag, select, save, print.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Lisa-like office seriousness and high price/early-adopter feel.

recipes.name: Wrist-scale pop
recipes.useFor: wearables, collectibles, fashion-tech, youth brands, modular identity systems.
recipes.palette: white plastic plus primary and candy brights.
recipes.type: simple, graphic, high contrast.
recipes.layout: circular face as poster; tiny information with big personality.
recipes.imagery: watch hands, straps, packaging walls, multiples.
recipes.motion: tick, swap, collect, stack, color rotate.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Swiss precision plus disposable second-watch attitude.

recipes.name: MIDI studio network
recipes.useFor: music tools, creative workflows, node editors, automation products.
recipes.palette: black, grey, LED red, synth blue, cable black.
recipes.type: panel labels, numbers, channel names, ports.
recipes.layout: modular devices connected by cables and signal routes.
recipes.imagery: keyboards, drum machines, cables, rack units, diagrams.
recipes.motion: sync pulse, sequence steps, channel routing, clock blink.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: hardware-first communication and five-pin cable logic.

recipes.name: Domestic game computer
recipes.useFor: learning games, family tech, playful software, console-inspired products.
recipes.palette: cream, red, black, TV blue.
recipes.type: cartridge label, pixel title, manual diagrams.
recipes.layout: console, controllers, cartridges, TV frame.
recipes.imagery: family room, cables, game boxes, custom chips.
recipes.motion: cartridge insert, power switch, sprite start, pause.
recipes.risk: importing grey NES nostalgia too early.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Japanese Famicom red/cream warmth and toy-like confidence.

recipes.name: Screen anxiety
recipes.useFor: cybersecurity, media critique, horror interfaces, command centers, network-risk products.
recipes.palette: CRT green, black, signal blue, flesh pink, military grey.
recipes.type: command line, warning labels, terminal output.
recipes.layout: nested screens, control rooms, tapes, feeds, system maps.
recipes.imagery: TV static, scanlines, hands on keyboards, broadcast equipment.
recipes.motion: signal distortion, boot sequence, access granted/denied, feed glitch.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: Cold War command logic and analog video texture.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1983 lens: the Lisa has made the GUI commercial, MIDI has
connected instruments, Swatch has turned time into fashion, and MTV has made pop
identity copyable. Give me directions that treat interface as lifestyle.

Give me three 1983-informed directions:
1. Friendly GUI office
2. Wrist-scale pop
3. Screen anxiety
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, interaction,
and what to avoid.

Critique this product as if it launched in 1983. Is it more Lisa GUI, Swatch
accessory, MIDI studio, Famicom living room, mobile executive object, or MTV
performance identity? What evidence supports that lineage?

objects: Sequential Circuits Prophet-600 and Roland Jupiter-6 as MIDI-era symbols
Synth panels, drum machines, MIDI cables
Leg warmers, rubber bracelets, Swatch straps
printAndGraphics: Style Wars titles and graffiti documentation
Thriller video stills and poster-like choreography
spaces: Subway yards and trains in Style Wars
Dance studio / steel mill / audition room in Flashdance
Parc de la Villette as event-grid landscape

antiCliches: Famicom confused with the later grey NES
MIDI represented as a modern DAW timeline
Hip-hop reduced to random graffiti texture
Thriller treated only as Halloween imagery without choreography and video-event structure

sectionMap.label: Architecture

sectionMap.label: Music

sectionMap.label: Film

examples.title: 1984: the graphical turn
examples.subtitle: Macintosh icons, LA Olympic color, Emigre type, cyberpunk language, portable digital audio, MTV ritual, and blockbuster identity make design feel newly graphical, public, and mythic.
examples.feeling: the screen learns to smile while the future learns to threaten
primaryLens: graphical user interfaces become popular imagination
environmental graphics turn a city into a temporary identity system
digital typography gets an independent culture
pop music becomes film, performance, fashion, and award-show spectacle
cyberpunk and tech-noir name the anxiety underneath personal computing

recipes.name: Friendly bitmap tool
recipes.useFor: creative software, local-first apps, personal AI tools, educational products.
recipes.palette: warm beige, black, white, cool grey.
recipes.type: bitmap-like sans, compact labels, menu text.
recipes.layout: windows, icons, tools, document surfaces.
recipes.imagery: mouse, sketchbook grid, Happy Mac-like warmth, printouts.
recipes.motion: cursor move, icon select, window open, tool stamp.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: low-resolution constraints and genuine usability.

recipes.name: Olympic graphitecture
recipes.useFor: events, festivals, civic tech, placemaking, wayfinding systems.
recipes.palette: magenta, turquoise, yellow, vermillion, acid blue.
recipes.type: bold wayfinding, multilingual welcome, supergraphic scale.
recipes.layout: banners, columns, flags, temporary structures, citywide rhythm.
recipes.imagery: sun, sports, Pacific Rim color, temporary architecture.
recipes.motion: procession, crowd flow, flags, wayfinding reveal.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: functional orientation plus celebratory inclusivity.

recipes.name: Emigre digital lab
recipes.useFor: experimental publishing, type tools, creative communities, design research.
recipes.palette: black, white, grey, early screen blue.
recipes.type: bitmap, custom, rough, system-constrained.
recipes.layout: magazine-as-lab, type specimens, immigrant/transnational fragments.
recipes.imagery: early Mac output, print artifacts, type experiments.
recipes.motion: page turn, pixel redraw, font substitution, printer output.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: exploration of digital constraints and typographic argument.

recipes.name: Tech-noir threat
recipes.useFor: cybersecurity, AI-risk interfaces, speculative fiction, dark product launches.
recipes.palette: black, neon red, chrome, wet asphalt, police blue.
recipes.type: neon noir wordmark plus dot-matrix tech labels.
recipes.layout: night street, club signage, surveillance frame, factory corridor.
recipes.imagery: leather, glass, red eye, machinery, smoke.
recipes.motion: scan, target lock, light flicker, hard cut, hydraulic reveal.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: noir typography plus 1984 machine anxiety.

recipes.name: Pop-world identity
recipes.useFor: music brands, launches, creator tools, campaign worlds.
recipes.palette: one dominant myth color plus black/white support.
recipes.type: cinematic title treatment, tour-poster clarity, album sleeve confidence.
recipes.layout: film still, stage, wardrobe, object, logo, single image repeated.
recipes.imagery: motorcycle, glove, wedding cake, big suit, uniform, logo patch.
recipes.motion: performance entrance, camera push, spotlight, pose.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: one world that connects sound, body, object, and media.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1984 lens: the Macintosh has made computers friendly,
LA84 has turned a city into color and wayfinding, Emigre has made digital type
experimental, and Neuromancer has made cyberspace feel dark and mythic.
Give me directions that keep friendly bitmap culture and tech-noir anxiety distinct.

Give me three 1984-informed directions:
1. Friendly bitmap tool
2. Olympic graphitecture
3. Tech-noir threat
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, interaction,
and what to avoid.

Critique this product as if it launched in 1984. Is it more Macintosh icon,
LA Olympic environment, Emigre type experiment, Ghostbusters franchise mark,
Purple Rain pop world, or Terminator tech-noir machine? What evidence supports that?

objects: Susan Kare icon sketchbook and bitmap fonts
Olympic banners, columns, and wayfinding pieces
Ghostbusters logo patch and proton pack
printAndGraphics: The Face issue 49 / "ELECTRO" cover and Industria
spaces: Emigre studio / early digital type lab
First Avenue / Purple Rain stage world
CD listening room and portable CD context

antiCliches: Hip-hop reduced to graffiti texture without style, sound, and dress
MTV reduced to random fast cuts without performance mythology

examples.title: 1985: the publishing desktop
examples.subtitle: LaserWriter, PageMaker, Windows, Amiga, NES, Live Aid, Air Jordan, rotoscoped MTV, and time-machine pop turn screens into publishing tools and brands into portable worlds.
primaryLens: desktop publishing joins screen layout to printed output
consumer platforms recover through stricter ecosystems and stronger identities
broadcast events and music videos become global design infrastructure
sneakers, consoles, and personal computers become lifestyle systems
retrofuturism and time travel make the past a design material

recipes.name: Desktop publishing revolution
recipes.useFor: writing tools, design apps, publishing platforms, documentation products.
recipes.palette: beige, paper white, printer black, cyan layout guides.
recipes.type: early digital serif/sans mix, page headings, font menus.
recipes.layout: columns, pasteboard, text boxes, placed images, print preview.
recipes.imagery: Macintosh, LaserWriter, paper stack, toner, page grids.
recipes.motion: drag frame, print spool, page output, font change.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: early Mac constraints and visible page-production logic.

recipes.name: Platform trust rebuild
recipes.useFor: game platforms, marketplaces, education products, app stores, creator ecosystems.
recipes.palette: NES grey, black, red, controlled box-art color.
recipes.type: clear labels, official badges, manual diagrams.
recipes.layout: cartridge, box, seal, grid, library.
recipes.imagery: TV, controller, shelf, mascot, instruction booklet.
recipes.motion: cartridge insert, boot, title screen, quality seal reveal.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: post-crash trust and ecosystem control.

recipes.name: Rotoscoped romance
recipes.useFor: music videos, storytelling apps, illustration systems, onboarding sequences.
recipes.palette: graphite, cream paper, blue live-action shadows, pop accents.
recipes.type: hand-drawn captions or minimal video titles.
recipes.layout: split live-action/drawn worlds, panels, portals, frames.
recipes.imagery: pencil lines, hands, faces, frame-by-frame motion.
recipes.motion: live-action-to-drawing transition, page tear, chase, reach-through.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: analog rotoscope labor and emotional transition.

recipes.name: Court-to-street sneaker myth
recipes.useFor: sports brands, wearable tech, youth campaigns, creator merchandise.
recipes.palette: black, red, white, leather grain.
recipes.type: bold athletic labels, winged marks, campaign headlines.
recipes.layout: shoe silhouette as hero object, athlete as myth, colorway as story.
recipes.imagery: court, street, locker room, close-up materials.
recipes.motion: jump, lace, pivot, ban notice, logo flash.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: athlete, color conflict, and logo origin.

recipes.name: Retrofuture bureaucracy
recipes.useFor: speculative fiction, civic satire, compliance tools, institutional critique.
recipes.palette: dirty grey, beige, duct metal, fluorescent green.
recipes.type: forms, stamps, labels, office memos, old terminals.
recipes.layout: cramped desks, tubes, ducts, forms, windows that do not help.
recipes.imagery: obsolete machines, papers, pipes, low ceilings.
recipes.motion: stamp, pneumatic tube, jammed printer, flickering fluorescent.
recipes.addAccuracyWith: bureaucratic absurdity and mid-century future logic.

promptSeeds:
Design this through a 1985 lens: PageMaker, LaserWriter, and PostScript have made
the desktop a publishing system; NES is rebuilding game-platform trust; Air Jordan
has turned a shoe into a myth; and MTV videos are becoming animation labs.
Give me directions that treat output and distribution as the core design problem.

Give me three 1985-informed directions:
1. Desktop publishing revolution
2. Platform trust rebuild
3. Rotoscoped romance
For each, explain the historical lineage, typography, color, motion, interaction,
and what to avoid.

Critique this product as if it launched in 1985. Is it more PageMaker/LaserWriter,
NES platform, Air Jordan identity, Amiga multimedia, Live Aid broadcast, or
Back to the Future retrofuture object? What evidence supports that lineage?

objects: VHS tapes and MTV broadcast monitors
printAndGraphics: Air Jordan Wings logo and campaign materials
Back to the Future title and prop graphics
Brazil forms, ducts, and bureaucratic labels
spaces: Live Aid stage and broadcast control room

antiCliches: Fully mature desktop publishing from the 1990s
Sneaker hype divorced from basketball and colorway controversy
MTV visuals as random fast cuts without technique
Cyberpunk if you mean Brazil's bureaucratic retrofuture
CD ubiquity as if it has already replaced all analog music
Modern social media charity aesthetics for Live Aid
